name: PowerShell Abuse Detection
description: Detects and investigates PowerShell abuse patterns including encoded commands, AMSI bypass attempts, download cradles, and obfuscated scripts. Riggs AI analyzes the PowerShell activity using the malware assessment template to determine intent. Malicious activity triggers script block logging extraction, host isolation, and SOC notification. Benign administrative usage is documented and closed.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: CrowdStrike Falcon
required_integrations.reason: Retrieve script block logs, process tree, and isolate endpoint if malicious

config.description: Triggers when suspicious PowerShell activity is detected (encoded commands, AMSI bypass, download cradles)
Decode encoded commands and classify the PowerShell activity against known abuse patterns

config.additional_instructions: Analyze this PowerShell execution for malicious intent. Consider encoded commands, AMSI bypass, download cradles, and process lineage. Determine if this is attacker tooling or legitimate admin activity.
config.description: AI-powered analysis of the PowerShell activity to distinguish malicious abuse from legitimate administration
Routes based on AI verdict and abuse flag count
Isolate the endpoint to halt any ongoing PowerShell-based attack
Alert the SOC team with details of the malicious PowerShell activity and containment actions

config.additional_instructions: Summarize the PowerShell abuse incident including the attack technique, indicators of compromise, and containment actions taken.
config.description: Generate an executive summary of the PowerShell abuse incident for leadership reporting

parameters.note: PowerShell execution on {{trigger_1.alert.hostname}} reviewed by Riggs AI. Determined to be legitimate administrative activity by {{trigger_1.alert.username}}. Flags: {{code_1.flags}}.

config.description: Document that the PowerShell activity was legitimate and close the alert
